questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the figure shown, ADB \& BEF are two fixed circular paths in a vertical plane. A block of mass m enters in the frictionless tube ADB through point $A$ with minimum velocity to reach point $B$. From there it moves on another circular path BEF of radius $\mathrm{R}^{\prime}$. And it is just able to complete the circle.
options: Velocity at A must be $\sqrt{4 \mathrm{Rg}}$.
Velocity at A must be $\sqrt{2 \mathrm{Rg}}$.
$\frac{R^{\prime}}{R}=\frac{2}{3}$
The normal reaction at point E is 6 mg.
questions.solution: For minimum velocity at $A$; $\frac{1}{2} m V_A{ }^2=m g R \Rightarrow V_A=\sqrt{2 g R}$ Now, $\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{B}}^2+\mathrm{mgR^{\prime}}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{E}}^2$ As, $V_B=\sqrt{2 g R}$ For looping the loop; $\begin{aligned} & V_E=\sqrt{5 g R^{\prime}} \\ & \therefore \frac{1}{2} m 2 g R+m g R^{\prime}=\frac{1}{2} m 5 g R^{\prime} \\ & \therefore \frac{R^{\prime}}{R}=\frac{2}{3} \end{aligned}$ And also $N-m g=\frac{m v_E^2}{R^{\prime}}$ $\mathrm{N}-\mathrm{mg}=\frac{\mathrm{m} 5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{R}^{\prime}}{\mathrm{R}^{\prime}} \quad \mathrm{N}=6 \mathrm{mg}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A body of mass $m$ is moving along a circular path of radius $R$ such that its kinetic energy at any instant is $k=k_0\left(\frac{t}{t_0}\right)^2$
where $t_0$ is an appropriate constant. Then
options: The magnitude of tangential component of force acting on it must be constant.
The magnitude of centripetal force acting on the body will be directly proportional of $\mathrm{t}^2$.
After a long time, the resultant force will make a very small angle with the radius.
The magnitude of centripetal force acting on the body will be directly proportional to t.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A block of mass m is pushed towards a movable wedge of mass n and height $h$, with a velocity $u$. All surfaces are smooth. Choose the correct statement from the following.
options: Block will reach top of the wedge if $u=\sqrt{2 g h\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right)}$
Block will reach top of the wedge if $u=\sqrt{2 g h\left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)}$
If the block overshoots P , the angle of projectile is $\alpha$.
If the block overshoots P , the angle of projectile is less than $\alpha$,
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform rope of line mass density $\lambda$ and length $\ell$ is coiled on a smooth horizontal surface. One end is pulled up by an external agent with constant vertical velocity v. Choose the correct option(s).
options: Power developed by external agent as a function of $x$ is $P=\lambda x g v$
Power developed by external agent as a function of $x$ is $P=\left(\lambda v^2+\lambda x g\right) v$
Energy lost during the complete lift of the rope is zero.
Energy lost during the complete lift of the rope is $\frac{\lambda \ell v^2}{2}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { F applied by external agent }=\text { Weight }+ \text { thrust force }=\lambda x g+\lambda v^2 \\ & \text { Energy lost in the complete lift }=W_F-\Delta K . E .-\Delta U=\frac{\lambda \ell v^2}{2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A hollow tube containing a ball is kept on smooth horizontal surface. Now, tube is accelerated horizontally and ball is slightly displaced from point 'A'. Let $N_1$ and $N_2$ be the magnitude of normal force applied by inner and outer surface respectively. Then, which of the following is/are correctly represent approximate nature of $N_1$ and $N_2$ Vs $\theta(0 \leq \theta \leq \pi)$
questions.solution:
From Work energy theorem
$\begin{array}{ll} m a_0 R(1-\cos \theta)=\frac{1}{2} m v^2 \\ v^2=2 a_0 R(1-\cos \theta) & ---(1) \\ m a_0 \cos \theta-N_1=\frac{m v^2}{R} & ---(2) \\ N_1=(3 \cos \theta-2) m a_0 & \\ \text { when } \cos \theta=\frac{2}{3} & N_1=0 \\ \text { for } \theta\gt\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{3}\right) & N_1=0 \\ N_2=m a_0(2-3 \cos \theta) & \end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle having mass $m$ and positive charge $q$ whirls along vertical circle of radius $R$. At the centre of circle, another positive charge $2 q$ is fixed.
options: The minimum velocity given at the lowest point to complete vertical circular motion is less than $\sqrt{5 g R}$ for motion in gravity.
For any value of velocity given in horizontal direction at the lowest point in gravity free space it will perform vertical circular motion.
Tension in gravity free space cannot becomes zero at any point during motion.
If the charge at the centre is removed and considering the motion in gravity tension will also be function of charge on particle.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{mg}-\frac{2 \mathrm{kq}^2}{\mathrm{R}^2}+\mathrm{T}=\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{R}}$....(i)

$-m g R+\frac{1}{2} m u^2=m g R+\frac{1}{2} m v^2$....(ii)
if $\mathrm{T}=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{u}_{\min }=\sqrt{5 \mathrm{gR}-\frac{2 \mathrm{Kq}^2}{\mathrm{mR}}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The potential energy of a particle of mass 0.1 kg , moving along the $x$-axis, is given by $\mathrm{U}=5 \mathrm{x}(\mathrm{x}-4) \mathrm{J}$, where x is in meters. It can be concluded that
options: The particle is acted upon by a variable force.
The minimum potential energy during motion is -20 J
The speed of the particle is maximum at $\mathrm{x}=2 \mathrm{~m}$.
The period of oscillation of the particle is ( $\pi / 5$ ) sec.
questions.solution:
$F=-\frac{d U}{d x}=-5(2 x-4)$
At mean position $\mathrm{F}=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=2 \mathrm{~m}$
$\begin{aligned} & U_{\min }=-20 \mathrm{~J} \\ & \mathrm{a}=-50 \times 2(\mathrm{x}-2) \\ & \omega=10 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec} \\ & \mathrm{T}=\pi / 5 \mathrm{sec}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
